rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee declarations in component type scopes for interface behavior; use implementation scopes for assign bindings.
Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks to contain AGREE contract elements.
generic_rules: Refer to component ports only through valid variables or outputs in component type contracts.
Declare constants and equations in component type annexes when describing interface behavior.
Use visible identifiers and feature names to define bounds and logical expressions in AGREE contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax includes `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` declarations within `annex agree` blocks.
Component type contracts should use `const` and `eq` for named constants and logical expressions.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level `assign` clauses in component type scopes.
Avoid referencing AADL properties like Compute_Execution_Time directly in AGREE expressions.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing-related tokens such as ms/us/Hz/Bytes
Model fusion and architecture redesign during repair
